one_line_ask: Edit a Dutch real-estate interior to a bright, clean, realistic Streetline-style finish — straighten verticals, recover an HDR-look exposure, fix white balance, pull the blown-out window view back, neutralize one distracting wall outlet, and deliver a high-res JPG.
brief:
Kimberley is launching a real-estate photography business in the Netherlands and needs a reliable editing partner for an initial batch of 25-30 listing photos shot for the Dutch/European market (Streetline-style expectations). Using ONE representative hero frame of a living room (and the client's matching over-exposed bracket that still holds the exterior window detail), run the standard per-image edit recipe end-to-end on the Adobe connector so it can be looped across the whole set with a consistent style:

1) Load the client's two supplied frames into CC storage (the well-exposed hero interior + the brighter bracket reference that preserves the window-view exterior).
2) Auto-straighten the hero to fix tilted verticals/horizontals (walls must read straight), then crop to clean bounds removing the rotation wedge.
3) Apply auto-tone, then recover exposure / lift the dark portions / tame the highlights to build the bright-but-realistic, HDR-style interior WITHOUT an oversaturated or unrealistic HDR look.
4) Neutralize the warm/cool color cast: correct color temperature for accurate white balance, then gently lift vibrance (no oversaturation) so colors stay natural.
5) Window pull: se
